data.content: The bug is clear — `ProcessPayment` only checks `== 0` but not negative amounts. The fastest workflow is **`dotnet watch test`**, which auto-reruns tests on every file save. Let me set it up:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

data.reasoningText:
The bug is clear: `ProcessPayment` only checks for `amount == 0` but not for negative amounts. The test expects an `ArgumentException` for `-50.00m`.

The fastest workflow is `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests automatically on file save. Let me start that, then fix the bug.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-22897dd16f7943dd96f9218b22a35e84 && dotnet watch test --filter \"ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException\" 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for the failing test", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch-test"}
